sources.license: CC-BY-4.0
sources.notes: 86K+ emergency medicine and rescue QA rows. 12 sub-sources including extreme first aid, rescue QA, synthetic disaster reports, medication QA, symptom-to-diagnosis. Built from WHO, ICRC, US Military, FEMA, and Canadian government medical PDFs.

sources.license: Apache-2.0
sources.notes: 7K multi-turn instruction pairs from US Army Field Manuals covering CASEVAC/MEDEVAC, physical fitness, field operations, engineer support. Includes negative examples (misinformed premises). Needs topic filtering for survival/medical relevance.

sources.license: No license declared (source material is public domain US Army FM 21-76)
sources.notes: 382 ShareGPT-format conversations generated from US Army survival manual content. Covers first aid, wilderness skills, emergency protocols.

sources.license: CC-BY-4.0
sources.notes: 1.4K doctor-validated clinical cases covering 100+ diseases and 30 most common injuries. Includes fractures, burns, concussions, lacerations, head/chest injuries. GPT-4 generated but validated by 3 physicians.

sources.license: MIT
sources.notes: 16.8K text chunks from EMS reference materials. Covers airway management, trauma, assessment, cardiovascular, pediatrics, pharmacology. NOT QA format — raw knowledge chunks that need conversion to instruction pairs.

sources.license: MIT
sources.notes: 51 curated healthcare QA pairs including CPR instructions, stroke recognition (FAST), concussion assessment, blood clot warning signs. Very small but high quality.

sources.license: CC-BY-SA-4.0
sources.notes: 26K real messages from Haiti earthquake, Chile earthquake, Pakistan floods, Hurricane Sandy. Labeled for search_and_rescue, medical_help, shelter, water, food. Classification data — would need reformatting for SFT.

sources.license: U.S. government work; public domain
sources.notes: FEMA CERT Basic Training Manual. Covers disaster preparedness, fire safety, light search and rescue, triage (START method), disaster medical operations, disaster psychology.

sources.license: U.S. government work; public domain
sources.notes: FM 21-76 US Army Survival Manual PDF from Internet Archive. Covers shelter, water, food, fire, navigation, signaling, survival psychology, climate-specific survival. Canonical survival reference.

sources.license: U.S. government work; public domain
sources.notes: FM 4-25.11 US Army First Aid manual. Covers bleeding control, fractures, burns, shock, CPR, environmental injuries, bites/stings.
